name: Bedroom
description: A private sleeping room. Contains a bed against a wall, a nightstand with a light source, a storage chest, optional crafting table, a central carpet rug, and a window on the wall opposite the bed. The ceiling is left open as a beamed roof.

required_furniture.rule: placed against a wall, 2 blocks long along that wall (resolved via style_variants.palette_overrides.bed → colored *_bed block)
against the wall opposite or adjacent to the bed
single block of @primary or @accent next to the bed head (and foot if medium+)
on the nightstand, plus at least one extra light source (lantern or torch) on a wall
a small central patch as a rug, not overlapping the bed footprint (resolved via style_variants.palette_overrides.carpet → colored *_carpet block)
only included on medium+ rooms (interior width and depth >= 8)

placement_rules: Place the bed against a perimeter wall, 2 blocks long along that wall.
Place a window on the wall opposite the bed (Alexander: 'Light on Two Sides').
Place primary lighting (lantern) on the nightstand, immediately next to the bed.
Place the chest against the wall opposite the bed, near a corner.
Leave the ceiling open: render the roof as Line-op beams between the wall tops, not a solid slab.
Center the carpet rug in the remaining free floor area; never under the bed.
lighting_ventilation: At least one lantern on the nightstand and one window on the wall opposite the bed. Aim for two light sources on different sides of the bed; on small rooms a wall torch substitutes the second lantern.

medieval.notes: Cozy oak-and-stone room with a red bed and a dark-oak beamed roof.

modern.notes: Clean quartz walls, white bed, gray rug, minimalist exposed beams.

fantasy.notes: Dark-oak chamber with a purple bed, purpur beams, and sea-lantern lighting.

python_skill_signature: pipeline.skills.bedroom.build(aabb: AABB, materials: Materials, style: str, **kwargs) -> list[Op]
